# The harness runbook

A **harness-neutral** projection of the code-homed workflow procedure. The
procedure lives in code, not prose: `_wire/spawn_contract.py::DECISION_POINTS`
enumerates every decision branch of the four workflows (each tagged `code` vs
`judgement` and bound to a backing verb), and `infra/block_chain.py` owns the
block SEQUENCE and the consent boundaries. This page states that procedure for a
harness that is NOT Claude Code ŌĆö the `hpc-agent` CLI verbs are the whole
vocabulary (the CLI is the invariant substrate; see
`docs/internals/harness-contract.md`). It carries NO harness-specific idioms; a
Claude-Code SKILL.md is one *profile* of this runbook, not its source of truth.

## How a harness drives any workflow

1. **Run the driver.** Invoke `block-drive` with the workflow's first block. The
   BLOCKS are the execution ŌĆö code does the SSH, staging, canary, submit, watch,
   and harvest ŌĆö and `block-drive` owns the sequencing table; the harness never
   hand-authors a block spec.
2. **Relay each decision brief.** When a block returns a brief, present it to the
   human VERBATIM. Deterministic code renders the brief; the harness does not
   interpret raw results or resolve the decision itself.
3. **Park at a consent boundary.** Before a block that mutates the cluster the
   driver PARKS and waits for a human greenlight ŌĆö it never self-advances past a
   consent boundary (the blocks marked *consent boundary* below).
4. **Commit a typed `y`.** A human types `y` (or a nudge). The harness commits it
   through `append-decision`, which records the approved input spec to the
   journal's `resolved` set. A bare `y` stands ŌĆö no gate is tightened to buy
   portability.
5. **Advance.** The next `block-drive` tick consumes the approved spec and moves
   to the successor block. Repeat until the chain reaches a terminal or a human
   branch (no deterministic successor).

The consent boundary protocol ŌĆö **park ŌåÆ typed `y` ŌåÆ `append-decision` ŌåÆ the
driver advances** ŌĆö is identical for every workflow; only the block sequence and
the decision points differ. A workflow with no consent boundary (see below) runs
its chain without a per-step human gate.

## `submit`

**Block sequence.**

`submit-s1` ŌåÆ `submit-s2` *(consent boundary)* ŌåÆ `submit-s3` *(consent boundary)* ŌåÆ `submit-s4` *(consent boundary)*

**Consent boundaries.** The driver PARKS before `submit-s2`, `submit-s3`, `submit-s4` ŌĆö each mutates the cluster and requires a journaled human greenlight. At each: relay the brief, a human types `y`, `append-decision` commits the approved spec, and the next `block-drive` tick advances into the block. The driver never enters one of these blocks without a committed `y`.

**Decision points.**

| Decision point | Shape | Decided by | Backing verb |
|---|---|---|---|
| `entry_path` | selection (choose one of several branches) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `suggest-setup-action` |
| `prior_run` | branch (switch on observed state) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `find-prior-run` |
| `axis_class` | selection (choose one of several branches) | judgement (a genuine break in control flow the driving agent chooses) | `classify-axis` |
| `throughput_plan` | plan (compute a parameterisation) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `plan-throughput` |
| `canary` | gate (a pass/fail the block decides) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `verify-canary` |
| `preflight` | gate (a pass/fail the block decides) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `check-preflight` |
| `validate_campaign` | gate (a pass/fail the block decides) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `validate-campaign` |

## `status`

**Block sequence.**

`status-snapshot` ŌåÆ `status-watch`

**Consent boundaries.** None ŌĆö every block in this chain is ungated and the driver chains through in code (a greenlight, when this workflow needs one, is committed once at start, not per block).

**Decision points.**

| Decision point | Shape | Decided by | Backing verb |
|---|---|---|---|
| `surface` | selection (choose one of several branches) | judgement (a genuine break in control flow the driving agent chooses) | ŌĆö (no backing verb) |
| `lifecycle_dispatch` | branch (switch on observed state) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `poll-run-status` |
| `resubmit` | branch (switch on observed state) | judgement (a genuine break in control flow the driving agent chooses) | `resubmit-failed` |
| `monitor_cadence` | plan (compute a parameterisation) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `decide-monitor-arm` |

## `aggregate`

**Block sequence.**

`aggregate-check` ŌåÆ `aggregate-run` *(consent boundary)*

**Consent boundaries.** The driver PARKS before `aggregate-run` ŌĆö each mutates the cluster and requires a journaled human greenlight. At each: relay the brief, a human types `y`, `append-decision` commits the approved spec, and the next `block-drive` tick advances into the block. The driver never enters one of these blocks without a committed `y`.

**Decision points.**

| Decision point | Shape | Decided by | Backing verb |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mode` | selection (choose one of several branches) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `aggregate-flow` |
| `partial_handling` | branch (switch on observed state) | judgement (a genuine break in control flow the driving agent chooses) | `decide-partial-handling` |
| `completeness` | gate (a pass/fail the block decides) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `verify-aggregation-complete` |
| `reduce_locality` | branch (switch on observed state) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `aggregate-flow` |

## `campaign`

**Block sequence.**

`campaign-greenlight` ŌåÆ `campaign-watch` ŌåÆ `campaign-complete`

**Consent boundaries.** None ŌĆö every block in this chain is ungated and the driver chains through in code (a greenlight, when this workflow needs one, is committed once at start, not per block).

**Decision points.**

| Decision point | Shape | Decided by | Backing verb |
|---|---|---|---|
| `path` | selection (choose one of several branches) | judgement (a genuine break in control flow the driving agent chooses) | `classify-campaign-path` |
| `stochastic_marker` | gate (a pass/fail the block decides) | code (a primitive's envelope records the outcome on disk ŌĆö verifiable, reproducible) | `validate-stochastic-marker` |
| `decide` | plan (compute a parameterisation) | judgement (a genuine break in control flow the driving agent chooses) | `campaign-advance` |
